title: C1 canonical source_hash
manager: One pinned, universal way to hash an FSL source so the answer is identical on every OS and toolchain — the cornerstone of the whole portability contract. Without it, "the same machine" is a matter of opinion; with it, two parties can prove they hold byte-identical logic, a certificate can name exactly what it certifies, and a registry can dedupe. It hashes LF-normalized, NFC-normalized, BOM-stripped UTF-8 bytes with SHA-256, so a Windows checkout and a Mac checkout of one file finally agree.
cs: UTF-8-or-refuse; BOM strip; CRLF/CR->LF; NFC; vendored SHA-256, `sha256:` tag; certificate subject = normalized-source hash; `proof_input_hash` over RFC 8785 canonical JSON (source + property + assumptions + checker-version, irreversibles #14 slice); ten vectors incl. CRLF/BOM/astral/NFC-NFD; provisional-tag sunset at v9.
